_about: Norwegian → English translation of exercises and trainer terminology used in the program. Keys are normalized (lowercased, punctuation stripped, single-spaced).

knebøy.name: Back Squat
knebøy.how: Bar on upper back. Brace, break at hips and knees together, sit down to at least parallel, drive up through midfoot.

knebøy konkstil.name: Back Squat (competition style)
knebøy konkstil.how: Powerlifting-style back squat: same low-bar setup, stance and depth you would use on the platform. Hit competition depth — hip crease below the top of the knee — and drive up.

knebøy mstopp.name: Pause Squat
knebøy mstopp.how: Back squat with a 1–3 second pause at the bottom — no bounce. Builds strength out of the hole and reinforces a tight bottom position.

markløft.name: Deadlift
markløft.how: Bar over midfoot. Hinge down, grip the bar, chest up, pull the slack out, then push the floor away and stand tall.

markløft konkstil.name: Deadlift (competition style)
markløft konkstil.how: Competition deadlift: stance, grip and lockout as you would on the platform. Stand tall with shoulders back, no hitching, no soft-knee lockout.

benkpress.name: Bench Press
benkpress.how: Lie on the bench, shoulder blades pinched and tucked down. Lower the bar to the lower chest with control, press up and slightly back toward the rack.

benkpress mstopp.name: Pause Bench Press
benkpress mstopp.how: Bench press with a 1–2 second pause on the chest — no bounce. Trains strength off the chest and grooves the competition press command.

benkpress ustopp.name: Bench Press (touch-and-go)
benkpress ustopp.how: Standard bench, no pause: light touch on the chest, immediate press up. Used for volume work.

skråbenk mmanualer.name: Incline Dumbbell Bench Press
skråbenk mmanualer.how: Bench at 30–45°, a dumbbell in each hand. Lower until your elbows are just below the bench line, press up.

franskpress.name: Lying Tricep Extension (Skull Crusher)
franskpress.how: Lying on a bench with a bar or EZ-bar held over your face. Bend only at the elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead, then extend back up.

leg extension.name: Leg Extension
leg extension.how: Seated machine. Extend the knees fully, squeeze the quads at the top, control the way down.

sittende lår curl.name: Seated Leg Curl
sittende lår curl.how: Seated machine. Curl the pad down toward your glutes, squeeze the hamstrings, control back up.

biceps curl mhantel.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
biceps curl mhantel.how: Stand tall, dumbbell in each hand. Curl up without swinging the body, squeeze at the top, lower under control.

enarms roing mhantel.name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
enarms roing mhantel.how: Hand and knee on a bench; the opposite arm hangs with a dumbbell. Row to the hip, drive the elbow back, squeeze the lat, lower with control.

flies mkabel.name: Cable Fly
flies mkabel.how: Two cables at chest height. Slight bend in the elbows held the entire rep. Bring your hands together in front of the chest, squeeze, control back.

modifiers.rpe: Rate of Perceived Exertion (1–10). RPE 8 = ~2 reps left in the tank. RPE 10 = none.
modifiers.rir: Reps In Reserve. RIR 2 = stop 2 reps before failure.
modifiers.amrap: As Many Reps As Possible — go until form breaks.
modifiers.tempo: Lifting speed in seconds: eccentric-pause-concentric (e.g. 3-1-1 = 3s down, 1s pause, 1s up).
modifiers.1rm: One-Rep Max — the heaviest weight you can lift once with good form.
modifiers.e1rm: Estimated 1RM, calculated from reps × weight × RPE.
modifiers.konkstil: Competition style — same setup and execution you would use on the platform.
modifiers.mstopp: With a pause at the bottom (typically 1–3s).
modifiers.sikt på å holde settene innenfor rpe-omr: Coach's standing instruction: keep your working sets inside the prescribed RPE range. If you're below it, add weight; if you're above it, drop weight.
modifiers.bruk første uke på å finne en passende v: Coach's standing instruction: use week 1 to dial in a weight that lands in the prescribed RPE range — the program builds from there.
